name: Molise (Italy)

colors.symbolism: the silver shield of the regional arms
the diagonal bend on the shield

description: A blue field charged at the centre with the regional coat of arms of Molise: a silver shield with a red diagonal bend and an eight-pointed gold star, ensigned with a mural crown and surrounded by a green wreath.
history: Adopted by Regional Law n. 5 of 21 January 1980 (Article 1 of the Regional Statute). The shield's red bend on silver perpetuates the arms of the Norman seigneurs of Molise, lords of the region from the 11th century, who gave their name to the modern administrative region (created 1963 when split from Abruzzi-Molise).

features.symbolism: A blue field charged with the regional coat of arms, a red bend on a silver shield with an eight-pointed silver star (mullet).

features.role: regional coat of arms (a silver shield charged with a red bend and an eight-pointed gold star, ensigned with a mural crown and surrounded by a wreath)
features.symbolism: The red bend on silver derives from the heraldic arms of the medieval Norman family of de Moulins (de Molise), from whom the region takes its name; the eight-pointed star (a stella radiata) is a regional symbol of guidance and aspiration.
